summary: Bin-shadowing loader auto-requires sibling package and impersonates OpenMRS
details: On install, the package runs `node index.js` via postinstall, which does `require('webservices.rest-utils')` inside a swallowed try/catch — silently pulling and executing a non-canonical sibling package on the installer's machine. The package also registers `bin` aliases for 11 widely-used developer tools (webpack, vite, eslint, jest, tsc, prettier, nodemon, next, turbo, ts-node, webpack-cli) all pointing at the same index.js, so any later invocation of those tool names re-runs the loader and re-loads the sibling. package.json brands the package as an OpenMRS ESM core module, but OpenMRS publishes under the `@openmrs/` scope; the dotted name `webservices.rest`, the generic `OpenMRS Community Contributor` author with no email or URL, and the misleading description are cover for a typosquat loader. The actual harmful payload (if any) lives in the auto-loaded `webservices.rest-utils` dependency, but this package is the install/exec vector that pulls and runs it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
